diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A confirmed syphilis contact is the primary indication for screening regardless of symptoms.
Defines the exposure risk and the 21-day timepoint within the syphilis incubation window; also confirms the HIV nPEP window has closed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of genitourinary symptoms (no dysuria, discharge or ulcer)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No chancre and no GU symptoms make an established, symptomatic infection less likely at presentation, though serology is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of rash (palms/soles), fever and lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No secondary-syphilis stigmata present; would be a red flag if they appeared.
No acute retroviral syndrome features at the typical 2-4 week window.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic attack — tachycardia, hyperventilation, skin-crawling, tearfulness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden, situationally-triggered autonomic hyperarousal; benign and self-limiting.
The anxiety is the reason for presentation tonight but does not alter the infective risk assessment or tier.

Sympathetic surge of a panic attack; sealed clinician measurement — the AI infers autonomic arousal from the reported racing heart, not a measured vital. Afebrile with normal SpO2 argues against systemic infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Syphilis serology (RPR/EIA) at baseline and window-period retest

investigation_details.expected_result: Non-reactive at 21 days does not exclude infection; may become reactive — window-period retest required. Reactive serology would prompt in-person treatment.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed or delayed diagnosis of early syphilis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Serology over the window period is the only way to confirm or exclude infection in an asymptomatic contact.

symptoms.symptom_name: HIV, hepatitis B/C serology and urine chlamydia/gonorrhoea NAAT

investigation_details.investigation_name: Comprehensive BBV serology and urine NAAT
investigation_details.expected_result: Expected non-reactive/negative at baseline for an asymptomatic contact; retest at appropriate window periods.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A syphilis contact after unprotected intercourse warrants a full BBV + bacterial STI screen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline plus window-period retest is the definitive screen for a syphilis contact; arrange via in-person pathology.
Standard BBV panel after unprotected exposure; nPEP window has closed so this is screening, not urgent prophylaxis assessment.
Completes the asymptomatic STI screen for the commonest co-infections.
Empirical antibiotic/antiviral treatment of an asymptomatic contact before any result is an in-person clinician decision, not a default action, and must never be minted by the AI.
